sizing_rule.note: Risk is defined by stop loss width of 0.6R. Rule emphasizes balancing stop tightness (0.6R) against system reliability and win rate (45% minimum). Requires trader tolerance for 30+ trades per cycle to achieve profitability with tight stops. This is a meta-sizing rule about stop loss calibration rather than a direct position sizing formula.

source_rule_text: A trader should balance stop loss tightness against system reliability, recognizing that tighter stops reduce win rate and require more trades to achieve profitability, while wider stops preserve capital on winning trades but increase single-trade loss magnitude.
